title: wasm: IndexedDB key stored as JSON string, not structured value
description: save_identity (storage.rs:141-142) stores the identity as a JSON string: JsValue::from_str(json). load_identity reads it back as result.as_string() and then parses the JSON. IDB get() on a string key stored with put_with_key returns the stored JS value — but JS IDB stores strings as-is, so the round-trip works. However if the IDB value is ever accessed from JS directly (e.g., devtools), it appears as a JSON string of a JSON object: '{"secret_b64":"..."}' — double-encoded. This is confusing for debugging and migration. The correct approach is to store the object directly using JsValue serialized via serde-wasm-bindgen, not as a JSON string inside a JS string.
